english: a form made by lining up pieces of wood and binding them together

etymology.description: Depicts bamboo slips strung together -> books; documents
etymology.interpretation: The character 冊(cè, 'a form made by lining up pieces of wood and binding them together') represents bamboo slips tied together with string, symbolizing early forms of books or documents compiled in this manner.
etymology.simplification: 冊 was simplified to 册 by removing interior strokes, forming a more straightforward and less intricate version.

examples.chinese: 这本书共有五冊(這本書共有五冊)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū gòngyǒu wǔ cè.
examples.english: This book consists of five volumes.

examples.chinese: 古代的册子(古代的冊子)是用竹片制作的。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de cèzi shì yòng zhúpiàn zhìzuò de.
examples.english: Ancient booklets were made using bamboo strips.

examples.chinese: 他在研究一件古老的册(他在研究一件古老的冊)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài yánjiū yī jiàn gǔlǎo de cè.
examples.english: He is studying an ancient tome.
